PRs missing backports.title: gh-144023: prevent follow_symlinks from being allowed with an fd of 0
PRs missing backports.body:
fd_and_follow_symlinks_invalid lets you use fd and follow_symlinks together if fd is 0.

This is incorrect, as 0 is a valid fd.

PRs missing backports.title: Update random combinatoric recipes and add tests
PRs missing backports.body:
* `random_combinations_with_replacement()` now treats elements as unique based on their position.  This matches the behavior of `itertools.combinations_with_replacement()`.
* Sync the signature for `random_product` with `itertools.products` using the parameter `*iterables` instead of `*args`.
* Add tests for all of the random combinatorics.

<!-- readthedocs-preview cpython-previews start -->

PRs missing backports.title: GH-91636: Clear weakrefs created by finalizers.

labels.description: new features, bugs and security fixes

PRs missing backports.body:
Weakrefs to unreachable garbage that are created during running of finalizers need to be cleared.  This avoids exposing objects that have `tp_clear` called on them to Python-level code.

Note that this is part of the GH-136189 change, split out to make it easier for back-porting.  This change is conceptually quite simple and is a candidate to backport to all older Python versions (this bug existed for a *long* time, perhaps in version 2.3).  There is some duplicated code between `handle_finalizers` and `clear_finalizers` but I think it's simpler just to leave that duplication.  In the "main" branch I'll clean that up.

<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-91636 -->
* Issue: gh-91636
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

PRs missing backports.title: gh-130595: Keep traceback alive for WITH_EXCEPT_START
PRs missing backports.body:
The WITH_EXCEPT_START opcode is getting the traceback from the exception object and then passing it down via a vectorcall, but only after decref'ing the traceback. 

But the exception object can have the only reference to the traceback. If the receiver of the traceback does something to cause the exception's trace back to be replaced, then uses the traceback, they access dangling pointer. The traceback shouldn't solely be kept alive by the exception object which can be mutated.

<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-130595 -->
* Issue: gh-130595
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

PRs missing backports.title: gh-130094: Fix race conditions in `importlib`
PRs missing backports.body:
Entries may be added or removed from `sys.meta_path` concurrently. For example, setuptools temporarily adds and removes the `distutils` finder from the beginning of the list. The local copy ensures that we don't skip over any entries.

Some packages modify `sys.modules` during import. For example, `collections` inserts the entry for `collections.abc`  into `sys.modules` during import. We need to ensure that we re-check `sys.modules` *after* the parent module is fully initialized.


<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-130094 -->
* Issue: gh-130094
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->
